explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Sources describe the challenge of biases in AI. The identification of trends is also mentioned in relation to fairness.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

relevant_sources.summary: This company blog post, "Hormel Foods Revolutionizing Food Innovation with AI," provides evidence for **fairness**, **governance**, and **transparency**. The post implies transparency by describing methods for introducing generative AI tools in ideation and mentions exploring AI capabilities. It also touches on fairness by highlighting the challenge of biases and identifying trends. Furthermore, the blog post supports governance by mentioning the critical need for external partners in AI adoption, suggesting vendor oversight and due diligence.
relevant_sources.title: Hormel Foods Revolutionizing Food Innovation with AI

governance.findings: Sources state the Board of Directors receives education on artificial intelligence to enhance their understanding of operational risks. The appointment of a Chief Technology Officer with prior experience in AI governance is referenced. Additionally, the critical need for external partners in AI adoption is mentioned, suggesting vendor oversight and due diligence, and AI for planning and efficiency is mentioned, implying governance over its implementation and use and surrounding the deployment of AI/ML algorithms.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This proxy statement provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. It indicates that the Board of Directors receives education on artificial intelligence to enhance their understanding of operational risks, demonstrating a governance function related to AI oversight and strategic decision-making.
relevant_sources.title: Proxy Statement 2026

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The appointment of a Chief Technology Officer with prior experience in AI governance suggests Hormel Foods is strategically focusing on establishing formal AI governance structures and leadership.
relevant_sources.title: Hormel Foods Creates Chief Technology Officer Role

relevant_sources.summary: This company blog post, "Hormel Foods Inside the Supply Chain Revolution," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The mention of AI for planning and efficiency suggests established governance over its implementation and use, while the discussion of AI/ML algorithms and their capabilities implies transparency and governance surrounding their deployment.
relevant_sources.title: Hormel Foods Inside the Supply Chain Revolution

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Sources state that Hormel Foods utilizes machine learning for data analytics to understand consumer preferences, improve marketing, and personalize online content by analyzing user behavior. The company describes methods for introducing generative AI tools in ideation, mentions the exploration of AI capabilities, and describes AI/ML algorithms and their capabilities. Additionally, Hormel Foods' stated use cases and perceived value of AI in product development, such as shortening research timelines and enhancing productivity, are described.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This article, "How is Hormel Foods Corporation leveraging AI in their internet use? - UMU," provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It supports this by stating that Hormel Foods utilizes machine learning for data analytics to understand consumer preferences and improve marketing, and that AI personalizes online content by analyzing user behavior. These descriptions imply transparency in how AI is applied and its capabilities within their internet use.
relevant_sources.title: How is Hormel Foods Corporation leveraging AI in their internet use? - UMU

relevant_sources.summary: This interview excerpt provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It supports this by describing Hormel Foods' stated use cases and perceived value of AI in product development, such as shortening research timelines and enhancing productivity. While the excerpt indicates capabilities, it lacks specific execution details.
relevant_sources.title: Thinking Outside the Jar: Hormel on Peanut Butter, Meat and Much More

summary.overall_findings: Hormel Foods' public disclosures address transparency and governance, detailing practices such as the utilization of machine learning for data analytics to understand consumer preferences and the appointment of a Chief Technology Officer with prior experience in AI governance. These are the only two of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars for which documented evidence was found. Drawing from 10 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
